# Extração: Beverly Hills M.D. Weight Loss

Best and worst fruits for weight loss.

In many cases, the problem isn't here, it's right here.

9 out of 10 dieters end up gaining back all the weight they lost.

This part of the brain is in charge of our body weight, how much fat we store, how hungry we feel, how fast our metabolism is.

Fortunately, the approach we've seen the best results with is actually much easier, because it's about adding in specific foods rather than cutting things out.

So let's go through exactly what these foods are.

This Gala apple has twice as much sugar as the Granny Smith apple.

So for losing weight, always go for green apples instead of red.

Oranges are loaded with the sugar fructose.

Too much fructose makes it harder for the body to turn calories into energy, so those calories get stored as fat instead.

Instead, go for strawberries.

A cup of strawberries will give you just as much vitamin C as an orange, with less than half the sugar.

And if your bananas look like this, throw them out.

I'm serious, a ripe banana can actually have more sugar than a glazed donut.

But here's the good news.

Eating that same banana while it's still green can cut the blood sugar spike in half.

Green bananas actually make a great snack for folks who are trying to lose weight, and that's because they contain a mild appetite suppressant.

Snickerdoodle. Why?

It's actually because of the cinnamon.

Cinnamon helps sugar exit the bloodstream through the back door, which makes it harder to store the calories as fat.

In fact, multiple studies have found that cinnamon helps with weight loss.

So when patients are trying to slim down, I tell them to add cinnamon to sweets and desserts whenever possible.

But what really surprises people is the other foods I recommend for weight loss, because some of them have a lot of calories and carbs.

We found that keeping weight off is less about how many calories a person eats and more about what their body does with those calories afterwards.

As strange as it sounds, our clients have been seeing better results from adding the right foods in than they ever did from cutting things out.

So let's go through exactly what these foods are and how they've helped many of my patients end the yo-yo dieting cycle for good.

What you're about to discover has already helped many of our Beverly Hills clients reach their target weight and more importantly, stay there for good without the yo-yo effect most people experience.

Now I know what you might be thinking.

A plastic surgeon helping people slim down without expensive procedures.

Isn't that bad for business?

Actually, the answer is no.

In fact, the buzz around this new approach has already brought in so many new patients.

The waiting list to see us is now almost a year long because it's easy, healthy, and it works.

The first clue we found for doing this was actually an Austrian medical case from 1890.

A skinny 14-year-old boy had suffered a brain injury and suddenly he began putting on pounds at an alarming rate, becoming dangerously obese in a matter of months.

What was happening to him?

A doctor at the University of Vienna noticed that the brain damage was only in one specific part of his brain.

But this part of our brains, it turns out, is in charge of one very important thing.

It's your body fat control area.

Now for years since this study, we've known this part of your brain has a major impact on weight, but nobody ever knew how it decides whether our weight should go up or down.

That is until recently.

Researchers have finally pinpointed exactly which hormone tells this brain center to start burning fat for fuel.

This fat burning hormone is called adiponectin.

I love adiponectin because the more adiponectin your body produces, the more fat you burn and convert into energy.

But here's where things get really interesting.

You've probably known people who seem to stay thin no matter how much they eat.

For years, doctors just chalked this up to good genetics.

But our lab tests tell a different story.

It turns out these naturally skinny people are simply the ones who produce the most adiponectin.

It's crazy no one talks about this because it also explains how people get trapped at a heavier weight.

Studies show that once you've gained weight and kept it on for long enough, your body is now making significantly less adiponectin.

And this is what locks you into that heavier weight.

When you lose weight, your metabolism actually slows down to the point that you're burning 20 to 30 percent less calories throughout the day.

That means on a diet, you could eat three meals a day, but your body will store as much fat as if you ate four meals.

So if you've ever felt like your body was trying to regain the weight you had before, well that's exactly what's happening.

And for people with the most adiponectin, their bodies are just burning through calories, especially when they overeat and even while they're asleep.

In fact, while everyone is talking about new drugs like Ozempic, almost nobody is talking about one of the most important things Ozempic actually does.

It turns out these drugs are major boosters of, you guessed it, adiponectin.

But it's clear there are some major problems.

Number one, it costs upwards of $10,000 a year, which insurance companies don't cover for weight loss.

And two, you have to get the injection every week.

That's a big commitment, especially considering you have to stay on it for life.

Within just a year of stopping, most people have already regained two-thirds of the weight they lost.

And now that these drugs have been on the market for a while, we're finding that a lot of the weight people are losing is actually muscle.

If you're over 40, this muscle loss is especially frightening because it increases the risk of falls, fractures, disability, and even a shorter lifespan.

With that said, the real question becomes clear.

Is it possible to shed the weight in a healthy way that preserves your muscle?

What I've discovered is the answer is yes.

There are actually some specific foods that can boost adiponectin naturally, so you can start seeing easier weight loss results.

The first food I want you to start eating is Marcona almonds.

These are the round, buttery almonds people eat in the Mediterranean.

Marcona almonds are packed with L-arginine, an amino acid that helps your body make more adiponectin.

And here's what's really special about L-arginine.

It's been shown to help you lose fat without losing muscle.

One study found that when people take L-arginine, 100% of the weight they lose is fat.

This is actually a trick organic farmers use to keep animals lean.

When they want their livestock to have less fat without losing muscle meat, L-arginine is what they add to their feed.

The next food is actually a carb.

It's the main diet staple of Kitava, an island in New Guinea where the obesity rate is zero percent, despite eating way more carbs than we do here in the USA.

The difference is in the specific carb the Kitavans eat.

Next time you're in the produce section, don't get the orange sweet potatoes.

Go for the white skin sweet potatoes instead.

While the orange ones are very healthy too, when the goal is weight loss, white skin sweet potatoes are the ones you want.

That's because they contain a unique compound that helps stimulate adiponectin production.

For even better results, sprinkle some cinnamon on top.

Cinnamon naturally supports weight loss by helping your body process sugar to cancel out some of its harmful effects.

But the biggest game changer of all is something most Americans have never tasted.

I only discovered it by sheer luck when I ran into my friend Bianca at a fundraiser.

I almost didn't recognize her because she looked so much thinner than the last time I'd seen her.

So I was intrigued when she told me she'd actually been eating more than ever.

Bianca had just gotten back from volunteering in Cameroon, and she was going on and on about this soup she'd become obsessed with during her time there.

A local Cameroonian dish called Napo.

Naturally, I had to have Bianca email me the recipe.

I figured my wife and I could get a little taste of Cameroon.

I never could have predicted that this exotic recipe would lead to the most important discovery of my career.

You see, Bianca's email mentioned there was one ingredient she hadn't been able to find here in the States.

It was a traditional Cameroonian spice made from the fruit pods of a tree called Dicrostachys glomerata.

I'm a nerd about this stuff, so when I hear about a spice I've never used, I start researching it online.

And one of the search results caught my eye.

It was a clinical study of 297 obese men and women testing how an extract of this spice affected their weight.

Now, it was a long shot, but if these folks had managed to lose a few pounds, it might actually explain why Bianca had slimmed down so much in Cameroon.

I didn't expect much, considering this was a simple fruit pod extract.

So when I saw the results, it was a shocking reminder of something we medical professionals often forget about, the amazing power of Mother Nature.

After just eight weeks, the study participants had already dropped not five, not ten, but an average of 24 pounds.

I mean, that is absolutely insane.

If a patient managed to drop 11% of their body weight over a year, any doctor would consider that a huge success.

And these people had done it in just two months without making any other changes to their diet or their exercise routine.

All they had done was start taking an extract from these Cameroonian fruit pods.

The researchers who ran the study couldn't pinpoint why it worked so well, but I saw the answer was right there in the study.

The fruit pod extract had helped stimulate adiponectin, the fat-burning hormone, by an incredible 21%.

Suddenly, the dramatic weight loss these people had experienced made perfect sense.

The next day, I showed the study to the person I trust most for a second opinion, my partner at the Beverly Hills Plastic Surgery Group, Dr. Peyman Danielpour.

He confirmed that these study participants had actually reduced their waist measurements by an average of four pant sizes.

And then he said something that really got me excited, I bet we could do even better.

How?

You see, when a client comes to us for a weight loss consultation, one of the first things we do is start them on a daily supplement regimen.

In addition to the foods I've mentioned above, we've found certain supplements that boost your body's natural ability to produce adiponectin, way beyond what regular foods can do.

After a decade of trial and error, we closed in on nine supplements in particular that make a bigger difference than anything else we'd tested.

As a result, most of our clients lose a significant amount of weight before they ever end up on our operating table.

We wondered what would happen if we combined this new Cameroonian fruit pod extract with the other supplements that were already working so well.

So we added this breakthrough to the regimen we'd been recommending.

And the results, I have to admit, even after all the transformations Dr. Danielpour and I have helped our clients achieve over the years, the next few months were very emotional for us because we watched as people who their entire adult life had never been able to keep the weight off, dropped massive amounts of weight, all while preserving muscle.

And what really amazed us was just how fast it was all happening.

In a matter of weeks, our clients were seeing the kind of results that people normally work toward for months or even years.

One client called us from the airport.

She said, you guys almost made me miss my flight to London.

Apparently, she had some trouble getting through security because she looked so much thinner than her passport photo.

Remember, the calories that would have gone to your waist and hips are now getting burned as fuel.

So it's no mystery why clients have told us they feel more energetic, more active, able to get more done and just enjoy the things they used to be too tired for.

Plus, now that you're not getting hit with those unhealthy food cravings, it's only natural to start eating better.

And that change alone is enough to feel leaner, healthier, and just overall better inside and out.

As a doctor, there's nothing more satisfying than seeing a patient slim down to their target weight while their health and wellbeing are actually improving at the same time.

The only problem was asking people to take 10 different supplements, including some really rare ones.

Even for such life-changing results, that can be a hassle.

So we started experimenting with ways to make it more convenient by combining all the ingredients together.

This was an ambitious project.

As the months went by, we were starting to think it might not even be possible.

We were close to giving up when finally we had the breakthrough we needed.

It's something called microbead technology.

Microbead technology can compress high-potency extracts of every ingredient into tiny, ultra-concentrated beads.

Amazingly, this allowed us to fit our entire metabolic regimen into one capsule, a supplement you can take just once a day to rapidly transform the way you look, and more importantly, the way you feel.

When it was finally ready, we started giving it to friends, clients, anyone who had ever struggled to lose weight and knew there had always been something blocking their progress.

After seeing people's results, dramatic results, sometimes after just a few weeks, we knew we had a game-changing discovery, something we needed to share with as many people as we could.

We called it Beverly Hills MD Metabolic Reset.

Metabolic Reset is the first formula designed to do what no other solution has ever attempted.

What sets this apart from all other solutions we've tried is that these results are not just significant, they're sustainable.

Finally, there's a tool available that understands weight management is a long-term goal, and that goal is finally achievable.

I'm gonna share with you more info on how you can try Metabolic Reset yourself in just a second.

But first, I wanna show you exactly how we're getting these incredible results.

You already know about the remarkable slimming power of one ingredient, the adiponectin-boosting fruit pod extract from Cameroon.

Well, for Metabolic Reset, we used a very potent proprietary form of this fruit extract called diglomera.

Compared to a more typical extract from the same fruit pod, study participants who used diglomera lost 31% more weight, an incredible result.

Diglomera was also shown to be better at keeping blood pressure and blood sugar in the healthy range.

One study found it can even help with maintaining normal cholesterol and insulin levels, which means this formula is not just helping you keep the weight off, it could literally be helping you enjoy more years of good health.

Metabolic Reset also features another adiponectin booster, an amazing plant nutrient called berberine.

Incredibly, while berberine has been shown to help people reduce their body weight and even shrink their waistlines, it's not a drug.

It's a simple plant polyphenol that Eastern cultures have praised for its health benefits since as far back as 3000 BC.

Unfortunately, most berberine on the market today is low quality and hard for the body to actually absorb.

In one study, 60% of berberine supplements tested failed to meet the potency standards of the US Pharmacopeia.

Even more shocking, they found that berberine supplements from expensive brands were just as likely to be duds, which means many people who have tried berberine probably didn't get to experience what this adiponectin booster is really capable of.

This is why we use a pure proprietary form called BerbIQ, the only berberine we've found that meets our high standards as physicians.

BerbIQ is harvested from Barbary plants in the Himalayas and then complexed with patented OMICS bio nanotechnology to help make it easier for your body to absorb.

Just these two ingredients alone would be a huge benefit for anyone trying to improve their body composition, but we didn't stop there.

Metabolic Reset also features L-Arginine, the muscle-preserving adiponectin booster found in Marcona almonds.

When you're slimming down, keeping muscle is key.

The amount of muscle you have later in life is linked to how long you can stay active and independent as you age.

For many seniors, that muscle mass could one day mean the difference between recovering from a fall and becoming injured, disabled, or worse.

That's why I believe L-Arginine should be a part of everyone's weight loss journey, especially if you're over the age of 40, because it can help ensure you lose the right kind of weight.

In a study of middle-aged volunteers, everyone cut calories and did the same exercise routine, but only one group was given a daily L-Arginine supplement, and the difference in their body composition was mind-blowing.

With the people who had not been taking the L-Arginine supplement, only 57% of the weight they lost was actually fat, but the people who were taking the L-Arginine supplement, amazingly, 100% of the weight they lost was fat.

They hadn't lost any muscle whatsoever.

This study even found that L-Arginine can support your most important muscle of all, your heart, with heart health markers improving by 30%.

Metabolic reset also features green tea extract.

One study showed it can help increase fat oxidation by 24%, and studies have shown that it can support significant natural weight loss.

For years, researchers couldn't explain why green tea was so helpful for weight loss, but by now, you probably know why.

That's right, it turns out it's actually a potent adiponectin booster.

The formula also contains just enough selenium to help speed up your metabolism by supporting healthy thyroid function.

And a growing body of research suggests this rare variety of cinnamon may even benefit your memory as you age.

There's also Stellaria media, a flowering plant native to Northern Europe.

And the best part is where those calories are going instead.

When a calorie is not getting stored as fat, there's only one thing your body can do with it.

Use it to make energy.
